prompt: Initialize a new TypeScript Node.js project for a todo application. Create package.json with name "todo-app", version "1.0.0", and scripts: build (tsc), start (node dist/server.js), dev (ts-node-dev --respawn src/server.ts), test (jest --runInBand). Include dependencies: express@^4.18.2, cors@^2.8.5, uuid@^9.0.0. Include devDependencies: typescript@^5.3.3, ts-node-dev@^2.0.0, @types/express@^4.17.21, @types/cors@^2.8.17, @types/uuid@^9.0.7, jest@^29.7.0, ts-jest@^29.1.2, @types/jest@^29.5.12, supertest@^6.3.4, @types/supertest@^6.0.2.

Create tsconfig.json for the TypeScript todo app with compilerOptions: target ES2020, module commonjs, lib ["ES2020"], outDir "./dist", rootDir "./src", strict true, esModuleInterop true, skipLibCheck true, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames true, resolveJsonModule true, declaration true, declarationMap true, sourceMap true. Exclude node_modules and dist.

Create the directory structure for the TypeScript todo app under src/: routes/ (for Express routers), middleware/ (for Express middleware), models/ (for data models). Also create __tests__/unit/ and __tests__/integration/ and a public/ directory for static frontend assets. Create a placeholder .gitkeep in each empty directory.

Define the Todo TypeScript interface in src/types.ts. The Todo type must have: id (string), title (string), completed (boolean), createdAt (string ISO 8601), updatedAt (string ISO 8601). Also export CreateTodoInput (title: string, completed?: boolean) and UpdateTodoInput (title?: string, completed?: boolean) types for request body validation.

Create an in-memory todo store in src/store.ts. Export a private Map<string, Todo> named todoStore. Export and implement these pure functions: getAllTodos(): Todo[], getTodoById(id: string): Todo | undefined, createTodo(input: CreateTodoInput): Todo (uses uuid v4, sets createdAt and updatedAt to new Date().toISOString()), updateTodo(id: string, input: UpdateTodoInput): Todo | undefined (merges fields, updates updatedAt), deleteTodo(id: string): boolean (returns true if deleted, false if not found). Export resetStore(): void for test teardown.

Create the Express router for todos in src/routes/todos.ts. Implement these endpoints using the store functions from src/store.ts: GET /todos (returns all todos, status 200), GET /todos/:id (returns todo or 404 with {error: 'Not found'}), POST /todos (validates title is a non-empty string, creates todo, returns 201), PUT /todos/:id (full replacement, validates title, returns 200 or 404), PATCH /todos/:id (partial update, returns 200 or 404), DELETE /todos/:id (returns 204 on success or 404). All error responses use JSON {error: string} shape.

Create Express error-handling middleware in src/middleware/errorHandler.ts. The middleware function signature must be (err: Error, req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => void. Log the error stack to console.error. Return 500 JSON response {error: 'Internal server error', message: err.message}. Export it as default. Also create src/middleware/notFound.ts that returns 404 JSON {error: 'Route not found'} for any unmatched route.

Create the main Express application in src/app.ts. Import express, cors, the todos router, errorHandler middleware, and notFound middleware. Create the app, apply cors() and express.json() middleware globally. Mount the todos router at /api. Add a GET /health endpoint returning {status: 'ok', timestamp: new Date().toISOString()}. Register notFound then errorHandler as the last two middleware. Export the app as default.

Create the server entry point in src/server.ts. Import the app from src/app.ts. Read PORT from process.env.PORT with default 3000. Call app.listen(PORT, () => console.log('Todo API listening on port PORT')). Export the server instance for graceful shutdown in tests.

Create public/index.html — a complete HTML5 page for the todo app. Include: viewport meta tag, title 'Todo App', link to styles.css, a header with h1 'My Todos', a section with an add-todo form (text input with placeholder 'What needs to be done?' and an Add button), a ul#todo-list for rendering todos, and a footer showing the count of remaining incomplete todos. Include script tag at end of body pointing to app.js.

Create public/app.js — vanilla JavaScript that connects to the todo REST API at /api/todos. Implement: fetchTodos() that GET /api/todos and calls renderTodos(todos), renderTodos(todos) that clears #todo-list and creates li elements for each todo (showing title, a checkbox for completed state, and a delete button), addTodo(title) that POST /api/todos and refreshes the list, toggleTodo(id, completed) that PATCH /api/todos/:id and refreshes the list, deleteTodo(id) that DELETE /api/todos/:id and refreshes the list. Wire up the form submit event and call fetchTodos() on page load. Handle fetch errors with console.error.

Create public/styles.css for the todo app with clean minimal styling. Include: body with font-family system-ui, max-width 600px centered with auto margin, padding 20px; header with h1 in primary blue color #2563eb; the add-todo form as a flex row with the input taking flex:1 and a styled Add button in blue; the todo list with no list-style and each li as a flex row with gap, padding, border-bottom; completed items with line-through and grey text; the delete button as a small red button; footer with muted grey text showing item count.

Create jest.config.js at the project root for the TypeScript todo app. Use preset ts-jest, testEnvironment node, testMatch ['**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts']. Add globals ts-jest config with tsconfig pointing to tsconfig.json. Set collectCoverageFrom to ['src/**/*.ts', '!src/server.ts'] and coverageThreshold global: branches 70, functions 80, lines 80, statements 80.

Write unit tests in __tests__/unit/store.test.ts for the todo store. Before each test call resetStore(). Test cases: getAllTodos returns empty array initially; createTodo sets id, createdAt, updatedAt and stores the todo; createTodo with completed:true persists the completed flag; getTodoById returns the todo when found and undefined when not found; updateTodo merges fields and updates updatedAt, returns undefined for unknown id; deleteTodo returns true on success, false for unknown id; getAllTodos returns all created todos in insertion order.

Write integration tests in __tests__/integration/todos.test.ts using supertest against the Express app (import app from '../../src/app'). Before each test call resetStore(). Test cases: GET /api/todos returns 200 and empty array; POST /api/todos with valid body returns 201 and the created todo with all fields; POST /api/todos with missing title returns 400; GET /api/todos/:id returns 200 with todo; GET /api/todos/:id with unknown id returns 404; PUT /api/todos/:id replaces title and completed; PATCH /api/todos/:id updates only completed field; DELETE /api/todos/:id returns 204; DELETE unknown id returns 404; GET /health returns 200 with status ok; GET /unknown-route returns 404.

Create a multi-stage Dockerfile for the TypeScript todo app. Builder stage: FROM node:18-alpine AS builder, WORKDIR /app, copy package*.json and tsconfig.json, run npm ci, copy src/, run npm run build. Production stage: FROM node:18-alpine, WORKDIR /app, ENV NODE_ENV=production, copy package*.json, run npm ci --omit=dev, copy --from=builder /app/dist ./dist, copy public/ ./public, EXPOSE 3000, CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]. Add a .dockerignore excluding node_modules, dist, __tests__, *.test.ts, .env.

Create docker-compose.yml for the TypeScript todo app. Define a single service 'app' using build context '.' Dockerfile './Dockerfile', with ports 3000:3000, environment NODE_ENV=production and PORT=3000, restart policy unless-stopped. Add a healthcheck that curls http://localhost:3000/health every 30s with 10s timeout and 3 retries.
Create .env.example with PORT=3000 and NODE_ENV=development. Create .gitignore for the Node.js todo app covering: node_modules/, dist/, .env, *.log, coverage/, .DS_Store, *.js.map.

Create README.md for the TypeScript todo app with sections: ## Overview (brief description of a REST + static-frontend todo app built with TypeScript and Express), ## Quick Start (npm install, npm run dev, open http://localhost:3000), ## API Endpoints (markdown table with Method, Path, Description for all 6 CRUD endpoints plus /health), ## Development (npm run dev with ts-node-dev hot-reload), ## Testing (npm test, coverage with npm test -- --coverage), ## Docker (docker compose up --build), ## Environment Variables (PORT default 3000, NODE_ENV default development).

Review the complete TypeScript todo app. Verify: all TypeScript imports in src/ are correct and no any types are used without explicit annotation, the Express router properly passes errors to next(err) in catch blocks, the store functions are pure (no side effects beyond the todoStore Map), the integration tests properly close the server after the test suite, and the Dockerfile COPY instructions reference the correct paths. List any issues found and provide the corrected code for each.

Write Jest unit tests for the TodoService delete method including non-existent id case.

Implement a bulk delete endpoint DELETE /todos that accepts an array of ids.
